name: The Quiet Frequency
description: Lena's resonance readings during the Armistice are different from the daytime readings. Not different in a way the instruments label as significant — different in a way a medic who's been running resonance checks for six years knows from feel. She wants comparative readings from the Grid node at night.
accept_text: She has the second notebook out — the instrument readings, not the observation log. 'Every night during the Armistice,' she says, 'my resonance instrument readings shift. Same direction every time — lower background frequency, higher lattice cohesion at the crystal depth readings.' She runs her finger down a column of numbers. 'This is six months of nightly readings compared to six months of daytime readings from the same positions.' She pauses. 'During the day: background frequency is in the range you'd expect for an active military zone. Aetherium discharge, crystal corruption, the standard noise of the war.' She flips to the nighttime column. 'During the Armistice: everything drops. The guns stop, the discharge stops, the corruption frequency changes.' She looks up. 'Here is the interesting part: the lattice cohesion reading increases during the Armistice. Not because the background noise drops — the instrument corrects for that. The lattice itself becomes more coherent at night.' She pauses. 'I want a reading from the Grid node during the Armistice. The node is a primary lattice junction — if the coherence pattern holds there, the implication is that the lattice responds to the Armistice in a way that is—' She stops. 'That is not how we understand the lattice to work. Lattice coherence should not respond to whether people are shooting at each other.' She meets your eyes. 'I would like to know if it does.'
complete_text: The Grid node at night during the Armistice is different from the Grid node during the day. You don't need instruments to know this — the warmth is different, more present, the way a room feels different when more people are in it than when it's empty. Arlen is here, or the part of Arlen that lives in the crystal is here, and the attention-quality of the node is heightened in a way you've learned to recognize as Arlen paying particularly close attention. You take note of what Lena asked you to observe. What you observe: the lattice at the node is clearer. The same warmth, the same resonance, but with less — interference. As if the background noise the lattice normally operates against has reduced. As if the lattice can hear itself better. When you bring the observations back to Lena she runs them against her column of numbers and is quiet for a long time. 'The lattice cohesion at the primary node matches the field readings,' she says. 'The pattern holds at the primary junction.' She closes the notebook. 'The lattice responds to the Armistice. The lattice is more coherent when the guns stop.' She is quiet. 'That means either the Armistice has a physical effect on the lattice — which shouldn't be possible, it's just two sides not shooting — or the lattice has a physical effect on the Armistice.' She looks toward the northwest. 'Or they are the same thing responding to the same condition, and we've been calling them by different names.'
